chapter_title.ar: باب تَقْدِيمِ النَّوَافِلِ وَ تَأْخِيرِهَا وَ قَضَائِهَا وَ صَلَاةِ الضُّحَى
chapter_title.en: Chapter 85 – Preceding the optional (Salāt) and delaying it, and its payback, and the Salāt at forenoon

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/417)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/417)
text: سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ فَاتَتْهُ صَلَاةُ النَّهَارِ مَتَى يَقْضِيهَا قَالَ مَتَى مَا شَاءَ إِنْ شَاءَ بَعْدَ الْمَغْرِبِ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ بَعْدَ الْعِشَاءِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al-Halby who said,

‘Abu Abdullahasws was asked about a man for whom the daytime Salāts were missed out, when should he fulfil them?’ Heasws said: ‘Whenever he so wishes to. If he so desires to, after Al-Maghrib, and if he so desires to, after Al-Isha’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once was asked about a man who has missed performing Salah (prayer) during the day. When is he required to perform the compensatory prayer for such Salah (prayer)? He (the Imam) said, ‘He can do whenever he wants. He can perform them after al-Maghrib or after al-‘Isha.”
